title: Malcor Brashin
content:

{{Quote|Brenn, I had such faith in you. I never thought you would turn out to be a traitor.|Malcor Brashin, to Brenn Tantor|<ref name="Force Commander" />}}
'''Malcor Brashin''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who was a respected [[Grand General/Legends|Grand General]] in the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Army]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Though his career had an unassuming beginning, he was converted into a fanatical supporter of the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]] after [[Non-huMan|nonhuman]] thieves [[Death/Legends|murdered]] his [[Malcor Brashin's wife|wife]] and [[Malcor Brashin's daughter|daughter]]. This event gave him the drive to become one of the [[Army/Legends|army's]] highest-ranking [[Officer/Legends|officers]], in which position he oversaw the development of the [[Battlefield Holographic Control Interface]] and served as [[Brenn Tantor]]'s superior throughout the young [[Officer/Legends|officer's]] Imperial career.

Brashin oversaw Tantor's early career in operations on the [[Desert/Legends|desert]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], the [[Mining/Legends|mining]] planet [[Ruul (planet)|Ruul]], and the [[Volcano/Legends|volcanic]] world [[Sarapin/Legends|Sarapin]]. He also commanded Tantor in later operations on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]], the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]], and a final mission on the hilly planet [[Abridon/Legends|Abridon]]. However, when Tantor disobeyed an order to wipe out a [[Abridon refugee camp|refugee camp]] on Abridon, Brashin had him [[Prisoner/Legends|imprisoned]]. Tantor escaped and defected to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], but Brashin later attempted to capture Tantor while Tantor was on a [[Mission to Trasse|mission]] to the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] [[Trasse/Legends|Trasse]]. Brashin was unable to keep Tantor from completing his mission and escaping. By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|fall]] of the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], Brashin was commanding the defense of the planet, where Tantor defeated his forces decisively.

==Biography==
===Rise to prominence===
{{Quote|Your Emperor thanks you for your cooperation.|Malcor Brashin, to an [[Hamman Flatt|interrogated prisoner]], after condemning him to death|<ref name="Force Commander" />}}
Malcor Brashin was an unambitious [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who lived during the reign of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], entering a military academy as a very young man. Even though he lacked ambition and was not outstanding, Brashin impressed his superiors as a competent and intelligent [[Officer/Legends|officer]]. During one of Brashin's deployments, his [[Malcor Brashin's wife|wife]] and [[Malcor Brashin's daughter|daughter]] were [[Death/Legends|killed]] by [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] thieves. This tragedy transformed him into a [[New Order/Legends|New Order]] zealot. Committed to destroying "unchecked anarchists and [[Alien/Legends|aliens]]," his new-found fervor for the Empire, combined with a string of victories, propelled his climb through the ranks of the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] until he reached its apex with the rank of [[Grand General/Legends|Grand General]].<ref name="TCSWE">{{CSWECite|I|92|Brashin, Grand General Malcor}}</ref>

===The training of Brenn Tantor===
{{Quote|These are dark days, gentlemen. I need my best officers to support the Empire now.|Malcor Brashin, to Brenn and Dellis Tantor|<ref name="Force Commander" />}}
[[File:BrennTantorAndBeriTulonAtBHCI-FC.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The Battlefield Holographic Control Interface]]
Something of a technophile, Grand [[General/Legends|General]] Brashin oversaw the development of the [[Battlefield Holographic Control Interface]], an advanced command-and-control interface that allowed an officer to command his [[Troop/Legends|troops]] on a [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] surface from the safety of an [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]]. Brashin employed a decorated training officer, [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Beri Tulon]], to recruit promising young candidates for training on the BHCI. During the [[Battle of Kalaan]], Tulon discovered a young [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] by the name of [[Brenn Tantor]] leading his squad after the death of his [[Unidentified sergeant|sergeant]]. Impressed by Tantor's leadership, Tulon issued him a battlefield promotion to [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] and ordered him to report to the [[Inquisitor (Star Destroyer)|''Inquisitor'']], the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] from which Brashin led his forces.<ref name="Force Commander" />

Once Tantor was aboard his Star Destroyer, Brashin observed Tulon leading Tantor through a series of training exercises on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. During this [[Time/Legends|time]], Brashin interrupted Tantor's training and reassigned Tantor's [[Squad/Legends|squad]] to track down the [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]] that had been ejected from the starship [[Tantive IV/Legends|''Tantive IV'']], with the [[Death Star plans/Legends|plans]] to the Empire's under-construction [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star battlestation]] inside. When the plans were discovered to be missing from the pod, Brashin oversaw a [[Battle of Tatooine (Galactic Civil War)|series of operations]] designed to track down the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] that were carrying them. Despite the efforts of Tantor, the droids escaped Tatooine on the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], a [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] owned by the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]], [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], and its destination was determined to be either [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] or the [[Ruul system/Legends|Ruul system]].<ref name="Force Commander" />

[[File:Brashin_sunset_window.jpg|thumb|right|Brashin meets Brenn Tantor for the first time aboard the Star Destroyer ''Inquisitor''.]]
During the course of Tantor's training on Tatooine, Brashin came to be concerned by the attitudes and opinions displayed by Tulon. In his opinion, Tulon held to an outdated and subversive code of ethics that had no place in the New Order. When the ''Millennium Falcon'' left Tatooine, Brashin took Tantor and Tulon with him on the ''Inquisitor'' to the planet [[Ruul (planet)|Ruul]], while the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] departed for Alderaan aboard the Star Destroyer [[Devastator/Legends|''Devastator'']]. Though the ''Millennium Falcon'' had in fact fled to Alderaan, Brashin's trip was rendered worthwhile when he discovered that [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] forces had captured a [[Ruul computer complex|computer facility]] on Ruul. Brashin observed approvingly as Tantor [[Assault on Ruul|retook]] the [[Computer/Legends|computer]] complex and, with the aid of his brother [[Dellis Tantor]], strung a series of [[Remote relay beacon|data relay beacons]] from the facility to a working [[Ruul uplink tower|broadcast antenna]].<ref name="Force Commander" />

Brashin took advantage of Tantor's excellent performance to promote him over Tulon's head. Now a [[Commander/Legends|commander]], Tantor would report directly to Brashin. Due to Brashin's concern with Tulon's instilling of more traditional military values in Tantor, Brashin reassigned Tulon from the BHCI project entirely. Brashin could now exercise direct control over his promising pupil without worrying about Tulon's negative influence, making it clear to Tantor that he had lost confidence in Tulon and that the Captain's mistakes were not to be repeated. Reviewing the information that the Rebels had stored in the facility on Ruul, Brashin discovered that the Rebel Alliance had covertly taken control of several [[energy collector]]s on the planet [[Sarapin/Legends|Sarapin]], a key source of geothermal energy. Brashin took the ''Inquisitor'' there and deployed his forces under the command of Tantor. Brashin and Tantor were able to [[Battle of Sarapin (Galactic Civil War)|retake]] the collectors with assistance from the technical skills of Brenn Tantor's brother, Dellis Tantor. In the course of the operation, Brenn Tantor, with the help of his brother, also managed to keep a [[Sarapin drilling platform|drilling platform]] that had been captured by Rebels, from causing destructive [[Groundquake/Legends|groundquakes]], after fighting through significant Alliance forces.<ref name="Force Commander" />

During the Sarapin operation, Brashin received the news that the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] had been [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|destroyed]] over the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], with the loss of nearly all personnel aboard. To fill the void, Brashin promoted Brenn to [[Major/Legends|major]] and Dellis to [[Colonel/Legends|colonel]], the latter of whom he transferred to [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]], a branch of the military better suited to his talents. Brashin and Brenn Tantor rushed to Yavin 4 on a [[Rescue mission on Yavin 4|mission]] to crush the [[Great Temple/Legends|Rebel base]] there. Tantor was able to raze the base and rescue Colonel [[Maximilian Veers/Legends|Maximilian Veers]], who had crashed on the moon after escaping the Death Star's in a [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]]. Tantor's performance drew the [[Eye/Legends|eye]] of even higher powers than Brashin, leading to personal congratulations from [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader. Over the course of the next three [[Standard year/Legends|years]], Tantor rose to the rank of general with Brashin continuing to serve as his superior, with their most notable engagement being the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]. During this battle, Tantor oversaw General Veers's assault on the Rebel Alliance's power generators. Tantor also supervised the landing of Darth Vader's attack force that was going to attack the [[Echo Base/Legends|Rebel base]]. Brashin and Tantor next moved on to the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]], where Brashin decided on three possible locations to become the site for the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star's]] [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|deflector shield generator]]. Brashin oversaw Tantor's [[Mission to Endor (Galactic Civil War)|exploration]] of the three sites and after that was complete he selected which of the sites was the most suitable. Even though the mission was completed successfully by Tantor, Brashin was angered by his reluctance to attack the native [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]] who lived on Endor's moon.<ref name="Force Commander" />

===From protégé to enemy===
{{Quote|Scanners show a refugee camp to the east of the base. Your Empire wishes you to eliminate it as a show of strength to our enemy.|Grand General Malcor Brashin, to General Brenn Tantor|<ref name="Force Commander" />}}
Unbeknownst to Brashin, Tantor had gained knowledge of his superior's other acts. Prior to the Battle of Hoth, Brashin had become aware that Tantor had used his position within Imperial Intelligence to look up records on his [[Tantor (Senior)|father]]. Tantor had discovered that his father had been killed not by Rebels, as the brothers had been told, but by the Empire itself in order to make an example of him after he lost a grain shipment to [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]]. Brashin reacted by sending Dellis on a mission to Ruul and arranging for his capture and imprisonment during its course. What Brashin did not know was that Dellis managed to pass the data and his suspicions to Brenn before embarking on what he believed to be a suicide mission.<ref name="Force Commander" />

[[File:Malcorbrashin.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Brashin reprimands Brenn Tantor via hologram after his insubordination.]]
This knowledge tortured Brenn. Despite his misgivings, Tantor successfully [[Massacre on Abridon|recaptured]] the [[Abridon Government Center|Government Center]] and an Imperial base on [[Abridon/Legends|Abridon]] and apprehended Rebel hero [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. Following the victory, Tantor prevented Beri Tulon, now under his command, from defecting. After the General destroyed the last Rebel base in the area, Brashin ordered him to destroy a nearby refugee camp in order to make a statement that anyone who tolerated the presence of Rebels would be destroyed. Brashin soon learned that, despite his removal, Tulon's teachings had remained with Tantor. Citing the mentor he had so recently captured, Tantor refused Brashin's order. Infuriated, Brashin locked him out of command frequencies and issued the order directly to Tantor's stormtroopers. In the aftermath, Brashin assigned Tulon to the [[Tarkin Detention Facility]] on Ruul and directed Tantor to report to the planet [[Kalaan/Legends|Kalaan]], where he would be court-martialed. In the same message, he gave Tantor the news that Dellis had been killed.<ref name="Force Commander" />

Upon arrival at Kalaan, Tantor was imprisoned in an [[Kalaan prison|outdoor cell]], where Brashin informed him that he would be tried, found guilty, and publicly executed in the morning. What Brashin did not know was that Tantor had contacted General [[Tyr Taskeen]] of the Rebel Alliance and arranged to defect. Brashin assigned [[Kalaan defectors|Tantor's own troops]] to guard Brenn Tantor. However, the stormtroopers were loyal to Tantor and [[Escape from Kalaan|released]] him, taking with them Tantor's fellow captive, Luke Skywalker, and the [[Tracked Mobile Base]], a prototype vehicle that contained a portable BHCI unit.<ref name="Force Commander" />

Brashin later encountered Tantor when the defector attempted to steal an [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|All Terrain Armored Transport]] from the [[Trasse Proving Grounds|proving grounds]] of [[Trasse/Legends|Trasse]]. Brashin appeared with his Star Destroyer, ''Inquisitor'', in the middle of Tantor's [[Mission to Trasse|operation]]. By doing this, Brashin trapped Tantor and Beri Tulon, whom Tantor had rescued from the Tarkin Detention Facility on Ruul, on the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid's]] surface. When Tantor and Brashin spoke to each other before the battle, Brashin expressed his disappointment in his one-time student, while denying a chance from Tantor to defect. A battle ensued, in which Tantor captured the [[planetary ion cannon]], which Tulon used to disable the ''Inquisitor''. Following the battle, Tantor escaped with the captured Imperial [[Walker/Legends|walker]] without further resistance from Brashin's disabled starship.<ref name="Force Commander" />

Although he failed to stop Tantor at Trasse, Brashin remained a prominent general within the Empire, even after the death of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] and Darth Vader at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. When the former Rebel Alliance, under the name of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|attacked]] the Imperial [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], Brashin commanded the defense of the capital world<ref name="Force Commander" /> alongside General [[Tal Ashen]],<ref name="Clone Campaigns: Tal Ashen">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns]]'' features the First Battle of Coruscant during the Galactic Civil War showing Tal Ashen leading the Galactic Empire.</ref> who oversaw the [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]]. The New Republic sent Tantor against Brashin, who hammered through his defenses and,<ref name="Force Commander" /> with the help of fellow New Republic general [[Rand Talor]],<ref name="Clone Campaigns: Rand Talor">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns]]'' features the First Battle of Coruscant during the Galactic Civil War showing Rand Talor leading the New Republic.</ref> captured the Imperial Palace, freeing Tantor's brother, Dellis Tantor.<ref name="Force Commander" />

==Personality and traits==
[[File:Brenn_Tantor.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Brenn Tantor, Brashin's favored student]]
{{Quote|This isn't over, Brenn.|Malcor Brashin, to Brenn Tantor, after the latter's defection|<ref name="Force Commander" />}}
Malcor Brashin began his career as a quietly competent and unassuming officer, with a predilection for advanced technology. However, that was radically changed when Rodian thieves murdered his family. Anger transformed Brashin into a fanatic supporter of the New Order and its doctrines. This fervor energized his career, driving him ever higher through the Army. It was this radicalism that caused him to reassign Beri Tulon when the Captain took moral positions that Brashin regarded as contrary to Imperial doctrine. Subsequently, Brashin also advocated extremely harsh actions against enemies of the Empire—a category he defined rather broadly.<ref name="Force Commander" />

Brashin treated Brenn Tantor kindly and took great pride in his accomplishments, though he remained somewhat distant. When leading Tantor before the young officer's insubordination, Brashin let him control the forces during many engagements, while he supervised Tantor's efforts by pointing out general objectives and relaying information. Even after Tantor's training ended, Brashin continued to merely supervise Tantor as the younger man took care of the tactics in his battles. However, when Tantor refused to follow his orders, Brashin's goodwill toward Tantor vanished. Brashin took Tantor's contrariness as a personal betrayal, and his kindness evaporated as he ordered his student's death.<ref name="Force Commander" />

==Behind the scenes==
Malcor Brashin made his first appearance in ''[[Star Wars: Force Commander]]'', produced by [[Ronin Entertainment]] and [[LucasArts]]<ref name="Force Commander" /> and released on [[March 21]], [[2000]].<ref name="GameSpotReview">{{GameSpot|url=reviews/star-wars-force-commander-review/1900-2536084|text=Star Wars: Force Commander Review|author=Greg Kasavin|date=2000-03-24}}</ref>
In the game, he was voiced by [[David Warner]].<ref name="Force Commander" /> In the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'', in standard game matches Brashin's name can appear as a leader for an AI Imperial<ref name="GB">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''</ref> Brashin was later briefly mentioned in ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]'' [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 34|34]], which was released on [[August 22]], [[2002]]. In the Fact File, his last name was mispelled "Brashen", and he was only mentioned as the one who identified three possible locations for the second Death Star's shield generator on the forest moon of Endor.<ref name="FF34">''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]'' [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 34|34]]</ref>
